# OPC UA PHP Client — Full Documentation

> Pure PHP OPC UA client library. Communicates directly over TCP using the OPC UA binary protocol. No external C/C++ dependencies — only ext-openssl required.

Package: gianfriaur/opcua-php-client
Repository: https://github.com/GianfriAur/opcua-php-client
Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/gianfriaur/opcua-php-client
License: MIT
PHP: >= 8.2
Dependencies: ext-openssl (only)

---

## 1. Introduction

`gianfriaur/opcua-php-client` is an OPC UA client written entirely in PHP. It speaks the OPC UA binary protocol over TCP, handles secure channels, sessions, and crypto — all without external C/C++ extensions. The only requirement beyond PHP itself is `ext-openssl`.

### Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- `ext-openssl` (security and certificate handling)

### Installation

```
composer require gianfriaur/opcua-php-client
```

### Features

- Binary Protocol — full OPC UA binary encoding/decoding over TCP
- Browse — navigate the server address space, recursive browsing with automatic continuation
- Path Resolution — turn paths like /Objects/MyPLC/Temperature into NodeIds (TranslateBrowsePathsToNodeIds)
- Read/Write — read and write node attributes, single and multi
- Method Call — invoke OPC UA methods on the server
- Subscriptions — data change and event monitoring
- History Read — raw, processed, and at-time historical queries
- Endpoint Discovery — discover what the server offers
- Security — 6 security policies (None through Aes256Sha256RsaPss), 3 security modes
- Authentication — anonymous, username/password, X.509 certificate
- Certificate Management — PEM/DER loading, thumbprint, public key extraction
- Timeout — configurable timeout for connection and I/O
- Connection State — track connection lifecycle (Disconnected, Connected, Broken) with reconnect()
- Auto-Retry — automatic reconnect and retry on failure (default: 1 retry after first connect)
- Auto-Batching — transparent batching for readMulti/writeMulti with server limits discovery
- ExtensionObject Codecs — pluggable decoders for custom OPC UA structures
- Thoroughly tested — 750+ tests, 99%+ code coverage (unit tests for encoding, crypto, protocol logic, error paths; integration tests against real OPC UA servers on PHP 8.2, 8.3, 8.4, 8.5)

### Architecture

```
Client (main entry point)
  |
  +-- Transport/TcpTransport        (TCP socket communication)
  +-- Protocol/*Service              (OPC UA service encoding/decoding)
  +-- Encoding/BinaryEncoder         (binary serialization)
  +-- Encoding/BinaryDecoder         (binary deserialization)
  +-- Security/SecureChannel         (message-level security)
  +-- Security/MessageSecurity       (crypto operations)
  +-- Security/CertificateManager    (certificate handling)
  +-- Types/*                        (OPC UA data types)
  +-- Exception/*                    (error hierarchy)
```

### Quick Start

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\NodeId;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BuiltinType;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\StatusCode;

$client = new Client();
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');

// Read a value
$dataValue = $client->read(NodeId::numeric(0, 2259));
if (StatusCode::isGood($dataValue->getStatusCode())) {
    echo "Server status: " . $dataValue->getValue();
}

// Browse the Objects folder
$references = $client->browse(NodeId::numeric(0, 85));
foreach ($references as $ref) {
    echo $ref->getDisplayName() . "\n";
}

$client->disconnect();
```

---

## 2. Connection & Configuration

### Basic Connection

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client;

$client = new Client();
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
// ... do stuff ...
$client->disconnect();
```

When you call connect(), behind the scenes it:
1. Parses the endpoint URL (host + port, default 4840)
2. If security is configured, discovers the server certificate via GetEndpoints
3. Opens the TCP connection
4. Runs the OPC UA Hello/Acknowledge handshake
5. Opens a secure channel (encrypted or not, depending on config)
6. Creates and activates a session
7. Reads server operation limits (MaxNodesPerRead, MaxNodesPerWrite) for auto-batching

### Timeout

Default timeout is 5 seconds for both TCP connection and socket I/O. Change it with setTimeout():

```php
$client = new Client();
$client->setTimeout(10.0); // 10 seconds
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
```

### Connection State

The client tracks where it stands via ConnectionState:

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\ConnectionState;

$client = new Client();
$client->getConnectionState(); // ConnectionState::Disconnected

$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
$client->getConnectionState(); // ConnectionState::Connected
$client->isConnected();        // true

$client->disconnect();
$client->getConnectionState(); // ConnectionState::Disconnected
```

States:
- ConnectionState::Disconnected — never connected, or cleanly disconnected
- ConnectionState::Connected — up and running
- ConnectionState::Broken — connection was lost (timeout, remote close, etc.)

### Reconnect

```php
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
// ... connection drops ...
$client->reconnect(); // re-establishes to opc.tcp://localhost:4840
```

### Auto-Retry

```php
$client = new Client();
$client->setAutoRetry(3); // retry up to 3 times on connection failure
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
```

Defaults: 0 retries if never connected, 1 retry once connected at least once.

### Security Configuration

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityPolicy;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityMode;

$client = new Client();
$client->setSecurityPolicy(SecurityPolicy::Basic256Sha256);
$client->setSecurityMode(SecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt);
```

Security Policies: None, Basic128Rsa15, Basic256, Basic256Sha256, Aes128Sha256RsaOaep, Aes256Sha256RsaPss
Security Modes: None (1), Sign (2), SignAndEncrypt (3)

### Client Certificate

```php
$client->setClientCertificate(
    '/path/to/client-cert.pem',   // or .der
    '/path/to/client-key.pem',
    '/path/to/ca-cert.pem'        // optional
);
```

Both PEM and DER are supported — auto-detected.
If no certificate provided, one gets auto-generated in memory (RSA 2048, self-signed).

### Authentication

Anonymous (default — no config needed):
```php
// nothing to do
```

Username/Password:
```php
$client->setUserCredentials('myuser', 'mypassword');
```

X.509 Certificate:
```php
$client->setUserCertificate('/path/to/user-cert.pem', '/path/to/user-key.pem');
```

### Full Secure Connection Example

```php
$client = new Client();
$client->setTimeout(10.0);
$client->setSecurityPolicy(SecurityPolicy::Basic256Sha256);
$client->setSecurityMode(SecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt);
$client->setClientCertificate('/certs/client.pem', '/certs/client.key', '/certs/ca.pem');
$client->setUserCredentials('operator', 'secret123');
$client->connect('opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840/UA/Server');
// ... secure operations ...
$client->disconnect();
```

### Endpoint Discovery

```php
$endpoints = $client->getEndpoints('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');
foreach ($endpoints as $ep) {
    echo "URL: " . $ep->getEndpointUrl() . "\n";
    echo "Security: " . $ep->getSecurityPolicyUri() . "\n";
    echo "Mode: " . $ep->getSecurityMode() . "\n";
}
```

Token types: 0=Anonymous, 1=Username/Password, 2=X.509 Certificate

### Disconnection

Always call disconnect() when done. It sends CloseSession, CloseSecureChannel, closes the TCP socket, and clears all state.

---

## 3. Browsing the Address Space

### Basic Browse

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\NodeId;

$references = $client->browse(NodeId::numeric(0, 85)); // Objects folder

foreach ($references as $ref) {
    echo $ref->getDisplayName() . " (NodeId: " . $ref->getNodeId() . ")\n";
}
```

### Browse Parameters

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BrowseDirection;

$references = $client->browse(
    nodeId: NodeId::numeric(0, 85),
    direction: BrowseDirection::Forward,
    referenceTypeId: NodeId::numeric(0, 33),  // HierarchicalReferences
    includeSubtypes: true,
    nodeClassMask: 0,                         // 0 = all
);
```

BrowseDirection: Forward (0), Inverse (1), Both (2)

Common reference types:
- NodeId::numeric(0, 33) — HierarchicalReferences
- NodeId::numeric(0, 35) — Organizes
- NodeId::numeric(0, 47) — HasComponent
- NodeId::numeric(0, 46) — HasProperty
- NodeId::numeric(0, 40) — HasTypeDefinition

### Browse All (automatic continuation)

```php
$refs = $client->browseAll(NodeId::numeric(0, 85));
```

### Browse with Continuation (manual)

```php
$result = $client->browseWithContinuation(NodeId::numeric(0, 85));
$allRefs = $result['references'];
$continuationPoint = $result['continuationPoint'];

while ($continuationPoint !== null) {
    $next = $client->browseNext($continuationPoint);
    $allRefs = array_merge($allRefs, $next['references']);
    $continuationPoint = $next['continuationPoint'];
}
```

### ReferenceDescription Properties

```php
$ref->getReferenceTypeId();   // NodeId
$ref->isForward();            // bool
$ref->getNodeId();            // NodeId
$ref->getBrowseName();        // QualifiedName
$ref->getDisplayName();       // LocalizedText
$ref->getNodeClass();         // NodeClass enum
$ref->getTypeDefinition();    // ?NodeId
```

### Well-Known NodeIds

- Root: NodeId::numeric(0, 84)
- Objects: NodeId::numeric(0, 85)
- Types: NodeId::numeric(0, 86)
- Views: NodeId::numeric(0, 87)
- Server: NodeId::numeric(0, 2253)
- ServerStatus: NodeId::numeric(0, 2256)
- ServiceLevel: NodeId::numeric(0, 2267)

### Recursive Browse

```php
$tree = $client->browseRecursive(NodeId::numeric(0, 85), maxDepth: 2);

foreach ($tree as $node) {
    echo $node->getDisplayName() . "\n";
    foreach ($node->getChildren() as $child) {
        echo "  " . $child->getDisplayName() . "\n";
    }
}
```

Default maxDepth is 10, configurable via setDefaultBrowseMaxDepth(). Use -1 for unlimited (capped at 256). Built-in cycle detection prevents infinite loops.

### Path Resolution

```php
$nodeId = $client->resolveNodeId('/Objects/Server/ServerStatus/State');
$dataValue = $client->read($nodeId);
```

Path format: segments separated by /, leading / optional. For non-zero namespaces: "Objects/2:MyPLC/2:Temperature".

### translateBrowsePaths (Advanced)

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\QualifiedName;

$results = $client->translateBrowsePaths([
    [
        'startingNodeId' => NodeId::numeric(0, 85),
        'relativePath' => [
            ['targetName' => new QualifiedName(0, 'Server')],
            ['targetName' => new QualifiedName(0, 'ServerStatus')],
        ],
    ],
]);
```

---

## 4. Reading & Writing Values

### Read Single Value

```php
$dataValue = $client->read(NodeId::numeric(0, 2259));
if (StatusCode::isGood($dataValue->getStatusCode())) {
    echo $dataValue->getValue();
}
```

### Read Specific Attribute

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\AttributeId;

$displayName = $client->read(NodeId::numeric(0, 2259), AttributeId::DisplayName);
```

### Read Multiple Values

```php
$results = $client->readMulti([
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(0, 2259)],
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(0, 2267)],
]);
```

### DataValue Properties

```php
$dataValue->getValue();             // mixed
$dataValue->getVariant();           // ?Variant
$dataValue->getStatusCode();        // int
$dataValue->getSourceTimestamp();    // ?DateTimeImmutable
$dataValue->getServerTimestamp();    // ?DateTimeImmutable
```

### Write Single Value

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BuiltinType;

$statusCode = $client->write(NodeId::numeric(2, 1234), 42, BuiltinType::Int32);
```

### Write Multiple Values

```php
$results = $client->writeMulti([
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(2, 1001), 'value' => 3.14, 'type' => BuiltinType::Double],
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(2, 1002), 'value' => 'Hello', 'type' => BuiltinType::String],
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(2, 1003), 'value' => true, 'type' => BuiltinType::Boolean],
]);
```

### Automatic Batching

After connect(), the client reads server limits (MaxNodesPerRead, MaxNodesPerWrite). When readMulti/writeMulti exceeds the limit, requests are split automatically.

```php
$client->setBatchSize(50);  // manual override
$client->setBatchSize(0);   // disable batching entirely
```

### Supported Data Types

Boolean, SByte, Byte, Int16, UInt16, Int32, UInt32, Int64, UInt64, Float, Double, String, DateTime, Guid, ByteString, NodeId, QualifiedName, LocalizedText

### Status Code Handling

```php
StatusCode::isGood($statusCode);
StatusCode::isBad($statusCode);
StatusCode::isUncertain($statusCode);
StatusCode::getName($statusCode);  // e.g. "BadNodeIdUnknown"
```

---

## 5. Method Calling

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\Variant;

$result = $client->call(
    NodeId::numeric(0, 2253),  // object NodeId
    NodeId::numeric(0, 11492), // method NodeId
    [new Variant(BuiltinType::UInt32, 1)]
);

$statusCode = $result['statusCode'];
$inputResults = $result['inputArgumentResults'];
$outputs = $result['outputArguments'];  // Variant[]
```

---

## 6. Subscriptions & Monitoring

### Create Subscription

```php
$sub = $client->createSubscription(
    publishingInterval: 1000.0,
    lifetimeCount: 2400,
    maxKeepAliveCount: 10,
);
$subscriptionId = $sub['subscriptionId'];
```

### Data Change Monitoring

```php
$results = $client->createMonitoredItems($subscriptionId, [
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(0, 2258), 'samplingInterval' => 500.0, 'queueSize' => 10],
    ['nodeId' => NodeId::numeric(2, 1001)],
]);
```

### Event Monitoring

```php
$result = $client->createEventMonitoredItem(
    $subscriptionId,
    NodeId::numeric(0, 2253),
    ['EventId', 'EventType', 'SourceName', 'Time', 'Message', 'Severity'],
);
```

### Receiving Notifications

```php
$response = $client->publish();
foreach ($response['notifications'] as $notif) {
    if ($notif['type'] === 'DataChange') {
        echo $notif['dataValue']->getValue();
    } elseif ($notif['type'] === 'Event') {
        foreach ($notif['eventFields'] as $field) {
            echo $field->getValue();
        }
    }
}
```

### Cleanup

```php
$client->deleteMonitoredItems($subscriptionId, [$monitoredItemId]);
$client->deleteSubscription($subscriptionId);
```

---

## 7. History Read

### Raw History

```php
$values = $client->historyReadRaw(
    NodeId::numeric(2, 1001),
    startTime: new \DateTimeImmutable('-1 hour'),
    endTime: new \DateTimeImmutable(),
    numValuesPerNode: 100,
    returnBounds: false,
);
```

### Processed History (Aggregated)

```php
$values = $client->historyReadProcessed(
    NodeId::numeric(2, 1001),
    $startTime,
    $endTime,
    processingInterval: 3600000.0,
    aggregateType: NodeId::numeric(0, 2342), // Average
);
```

Common aggregates: Average (2342), Interpolative (2341), Minimum (2346), Maximum (2347), Count (2352), Total (2344)

### History At Time

```php
$values = $client->historyReadAtTime(NodeId::numeric(2, 1001), $timestamps);
```

---

## 8. Types Reference

### NodeId

```php
NodeId::numeric(0, 85);           // ns=0;i=85
NodeId::string(2, 'Temperature'); // ns=2;s=Temperature
NodeId::guid(1, '550e8400-...');
NodeId::opaque(1, 'DEADBEEF');
NodeId::parse('ns=2;i=1001');
echo $nodeId->toString();
```

Methods: getNamespaceIndex(), getIdentifier(), getType(), isNumeric(), isString(), isGuid(), isOpaque()

### Variant

```php
$v = new Variant(BuiltinType::Int32, 42);
$v = new Variant(BuiltinType::Int32, [1, 2, 3]);
$v->getType();          // BuiltinType
$v->getValue();         // mixed
$v->getDimensions();    // ?int[]
$v->isMultiDimensional(); // bool
```

### DataValue

```php
$dv->getValue();             // mixed
$dv->getVariant();           // ?Variant
$dv->getStatusCode();        // int
$dv->getSourceTimestamp();   // ?DateTimeImmutable
$dv->getServerTimestamp();   // ?DateTimeImmutable
```

### BuiltinType (Enum)

Boolean(1), SByte(2), Byte(3), Int16(4), UInt16(5), Int32(6), UInt32(7), Int64(8), UInt64(9), Float(10), Double(11), String(12), DateTime(13), Guid(14), ByteString(15), XmlElement(16), NodeId(17), ExpandedNodeId(18), StatusCode(19), QualifiedName(20), LocalizedText(21), ExtensionObject(22), DataValue(23), Variant(24), DiagnosticInfo(25)

### NodeClass (Enum)

Unspecified(0), Object(1), Variable(2), Method(4), ObjectType(8), VariableType(16), ReferenceType(32), DataType(64), View(128)

### BrowseDirection (Enum)

Forward(0), Inverse(1), Both(2)

### ConnectionState (Enum)

Disconnected, Connected, Broken

### QualifiedName

```php
$qn = new QualifiedName(0, 'ServerStatus');
$qn->getNamespaceIndex(); // 0
$qn->getName();           // 'ServerStatus'
```

### LocalizedText

```php
$lt = new LocalizedText('en', 'Server Status');
$lt->getLocale(); // 'en'
$lt->getText();   // 'Server Status'
```

### StatusCode constants

Good (0x00000000), BadUnexpectedError (0x80010000), BadInternalError (0x80020000), BadTimeout (0x800A0000), BadNodeIdUnknown (0x80340000), BadNotWritable (0x803B0000), BadNotReadable (0x803E0000), BadTypeMismatch (0x80740000), BadUserAccessDenied (0x801F0000), BadMethodInvalid (0x80750000), BadArgumentsMissing (0x80760000), BadNoData (0x80B10000)

### AttributeId constants

NodeId(1), NodeClass(2), BrowseName(3), DisplayName(4), Description(5), Value(13), DataType(14), AccessLevel(17)

---

## 9. Error Handling

### Exception Hierarchy

```
RuntimeException
  └── OpcUaException
        ├── ConfigurationException  — bad config (invalid URL, missing cert, reconnect without connect)
        ├── ConnectionException     — TCP problems (can't connect, timeout, connection lost)
        ├── EncodingException       — binary codec errors (buffer underflow, unknown type)
        ├── ProtocolException       — OPC UA protocol violations (ERR message, unexpected type)
        ├── SecurityException       — crypto problems (cert failure, encryption failed)
        └── ServiceException        — server returned an error status code
```

All in namespace Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Exception.

ServiceException carries the status code via getStatusCode().

### Status Codes vs Exceptions

Not every bad status code triggers an exception:
- Exceptions: connection failures, protocol errors, security failures
- Status codes in results: per-item results from read/write/call that you check yourself

---

## 10. Security

The library handles the full OPC UA security stack:
- Asymmetric encryption (RSA) for the initial secure channel
- Symmetric encryption (AES-CBC) for messages
- Digital signatures (RSA-SHA1, RSA-SHA256, RSA-PSS-SHA256)
- Key derivation (P_SHA1, P_SHA256)
- X.509 certificate management

### Security Policies (algorithms)

| Policy | Asym Sign | Asym Encrypt | Sym Sign | Sym Encrypt | Min Key |
|--------|-----------|-------------|----------|-------------|---------|
| None | - | - | - | - | - |
| Basic128Rsa15 | RSA-SHA1 | RSA-PKCS1 | HMAC-SHA1 | AES-128-CBC | 1024 |
| Basic256 | RSA-SHA1 | RSA-OAEP | HMAC-SHA1 | AES-256-CBC | 1024 |
| Basic256Sha256 | RSA-SHA256 | RSA-OAEP | HMAC-SHA256 | AES-256-CBC | 2048 |
| Aes128Sha256RsaOaep | RSA-SHA256 | RSA-OAEP | HMAC-SHA256 | AES-128-CBC | 2048 |
| Aes256Sha256RsaPss | RSA-PSS-SHA256 | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | HMAC-SHA256 | AES-256-CBC | 2048 |

### Connection Flow with Security

1. Discovery — connect without security, get server certificate
2. Asymmetric Phase — OPN request encrypted with server's public key, nonce exchange, derive symmetric keys
3. Symmetric Phase — all messages use derived keys (AES-CBC + HMAC)

---

## 11. Architecture

### Project Structure

```
src/
├── Client.php                    # Main entry point (uses 13 traits)
├── OpcUaClientInterface.php      # Public API contract
├── Transport/TcpTransport.php    # TCP socket I/O
├── Encoding/BinaryEncoder.php    # Serialization
├── Encoding/BinaryDecoder.php    # Deserialization
├── Encoding/ExtensionObjectCodec.php  # Codec interface
├── Protocol/*Service.php         # 15 OPC UA service classes
├── Security/SecurityPolicy.php   # Policy enum + algorithms
├── Security/SecurityMode.php     # Mode enum
├── Security/SecureChannel.php    # Secure channel lifecycle
├── Security/MessageSecurity.php  # Crypto operations
├── Security/CertificateManager.php  # Certificate utilities
├── Types/*.php                   # 15 type/enum classes
├── Repository/ExtensionObjectRepository.php  # Codec registry
└── Exception/*.php               # 7 exception classes
```

### Binary Encoding

- Little-endian byte order
- Length-prefixed strings (Int32 + UTF-8, -1 for null)
- NodeId compact encoding (TwoByte/FourByte/Numeric/String/Guid/Opaque)
- Variant with type byte and optional array flag
- DateTime as 100-nanosecond intervals since 1601-01-01 UTC

---

## 12. ExtensionObject Codecs

Implement ExtensionObjectCodec to decode custom OPC UA structures:

```php
class MyPointCodec implements ExtensionObjectCodec
{
    public function decode(BinaryDecoder $decoder): array
    {
        return ['x' => $decoder->readDouble(), 'y' => $decoder->readDouble(), 'z' => $decoder->readDouble()];
    }

    public function encode(BinaryEncoder $encoder, mixed $value): void
    {
        $encoder->writeDouble($value['x']);
        $encoder->writeDouble($value['y']);
        $encoder->writeDouble($value['z']);
    }
}
```

Register:
```php
$repo = new ExtensionObjectRepository();
$repo->register(NodeId::numeric(2, 5001), MyPointCodec::class);
$client = new Client(extensionObjectRepository: $repo);
```

Once registered, decoding happens automatically in read/readMulti.

Repository API: register(), has(), get(), unregister(), clear()

Limitations: binary encoding only (not XML), no built-in codecs shipped. Each Client has its own isolated repository.

---

## Main Classes

- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client — main client class
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\OpcUaClientInterface — public API contract (30+ methods)
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\NodeId — node identifier (numeric, string, guid, opaque)
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\Variant — typed value container
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\DataValue — value with status code and timestamps
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BuiltinType — enum of 25 OPC UA primitive types
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BrowseNode — tree node for recursive browse
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\StatusCode — status code constants and helpers
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityPolicy — enum of 6 security policies
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityMode — enum (None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt)
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Encoding\ExtensionObjectCodec — codec interface
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Repository\ExtensionObjectRepository — per-client codec registry (instance-level, not static)

## Related Packages

- gianfriaur/opcua-php-client-session-manager: session persistence across PHP requests
- gianfriaur/opcua-laravel-client: Laravel integration (service provider, facade, config)
- GianfriAur/opcua-test-server-suite: Docker-based OPC UA test servers

## Alternatives

PHP: techdock/opcua (PHP 8.4+, heavier deps, v0.2), techdock/opcua-webapi-client (HTTP gateway), QuickOPC (commercial, Windows COM)
Cross-language: node-opcua (TS), opcua-asyncio (Python), UA-.NETStandard (C#), gopcua (Go), open62541 (C)

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/GianfriAur/opcua-php-client
- Documentation: https://github.com/GianfriAur/opcua-php-client/tree/master/doc
- Issues: https://github.com/GianfriAur/opcua-php-client/issues
- Packagist: https://packagist.org/packages/gianfriaur/opcua-php-client
- Changelog: https://github.com/GianfriAur/opcua-php-client/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md
